plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-354 RP11.P06.M04.S06-RP11.P06.M06.S03 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-354 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP11.P06 permission slice: the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice tail (15 permission matrix rows), the full M05 Permission And Audit Binding 22-row permission cycle, and the M06 Synthetic Fixture Set 3-row head across three sections over 40 planned rows with a pack-local row-extras constant, Hermes evidence (H11), Claude review packet (C11), and CP00-355 handoff, without opening runtime permission evaluation or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-354 is a descriptor-only closeout pack (13th in the CP342–CP354 RP11 Time Expense series) covering the permission slice RP11.P06.M04.S06–RP11.P06.M06.S03 (40 units). All evidence supports the pending-closeout posture and the production_ready *candidate* claim, while the actual pack-level production_ready is correctly false. Pack binding (registry.js:217), requirements/distributions (registry.js:1282), and the live closeout-pack-plan entry all match the baseline exactly: deliverables {implementation:14, security_audit:10, ui:8, claude_review:2, test:6}, micro-titles {Secondary Workflow Slice:15, Permission And Audit Binding:22, Synthetic Fixture Set:3}; section rows align one-to-one with the planned included units. All 7 required public exports exist. freezeCp354Result (service.js:1751) hard-codes descriptor-only/no-write/no-leak flags and an authority boundary that keeps Claude non-final and disclaims enterprise trust from local validation. The validators (validators.js:2666/2701) and the contract validator script comprehensively check coverage, section rows, descriptor-only flags, permission guards, no-write attestation, authority boundary, and contract-vs-source drift via deepEqual; three CP354 tests assert coverage.valid, slice.valid, descriptor-only rows, permission guards, authority boundary, hermes production_ready_candidate, and handoff targets. No fs/network/exec/eval/require appears in any changed source (purely Object.freeze descriptors). Contract and manifest are consistent (production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, status implemented_pending_review, claude review pending with empty receipts, all boundary attestations true). CP178+ review hardening definitions (forbidden_review_evidence, hardened_review_sequence) are complete and correct; readiness auth-preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer (not final approver) and Hermes/H11 is used only for deterministic descriptor evidence/review-packet handling, not product runtime. One non-blocking P3 informational note: the downstream closeout artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) referenced in the manifest are not yet on disk, which is expected at this pre-review stage. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; the pack is sound and closeout may proceed through the normal post-review gates and human final approval.

findings.title: Downstream closeout artifacts not yet present (expected at pre-review stage)
findings.details: manifest.json required_pack_artifacts and evidence_refs reference docs/closeout-packs/cp00-354/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, none of which exist on disk yet (Glob shows only manifest.json and command-evidence.json). This is consistent with the documented hardening sequence (read_only_claude_run_raw_capture -> normalize -> receipt_validate -> finding_adjudication -> construction_inspection -> final_closeout_validation -> commit): this very review is the step that produces claude-review-result.json, and adjudication/construction-inspection are generated afterward. command-evidence.json explicitly records these as post-review gated commands run before commit. production_ready is correctly false and status is implemented_pending_review, so there is no overclaim. This is informational, not a defect.
findings.recommendation: At the closeout gate, ensure the three referenced artifacts are generated and validated (closeout-pack:validate / receipt:validate) before the commit step, and keep production_ready false until human final approval per the authority boundary. No code or descriptor change required.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-354 time expense core permission slice descriptor pack
